title: Scotland airspace modernisation: How flight paths change

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: What Scottish Airspace Modernisation plans could mean for flight paths around Edinburgh and Glasgow airports

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Scottish Airspace Modernisation: What it will mean for flight paths around Edinburgh and Glasgow airports? With the public consultation period closing soon, how could those living near the two key Scottish airports be affected by the modernisation plans?

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: As part of a wider scheme to bring the UK airspace up to the standard required by modern aviation, two Scottish airports are undertaking a review of flight paths for arriving and departing traffic. The project aims to reduce flight times, alleviate congestion, and mitigate harmful carbon dioxide emissions from aircraft, both in the vicinity of the two airports and across a wider area of Scotland.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Scotland airspace modernisation: How flight paths change
